body.title: Scaffold Hubert rack device
body.result: HubertDevice+HubertShim created; registers in flat-file registry; BOOT_READY logged; appears in /api/device/list (e0bf9312)
body.intention: I intend that Hubert exists as a rack device, appears in the device list, and gets a stub fascia tab — making him the named owner of build process and infrastructure.
body.description:
Create devices/hubert/ with BaseDevice/BaseShim structure. Hubert owns: lab/, all tickets, decisions, outcomes, palace browser, goals, rack infrastructure. Phase 1 is scaffold only — device starts, registers in flat-file registry, appears in rack and web UI device list with a stub fascia page.

**Affected files:** devices/hubert/__init__.py, devices/hubert/device.py, devices/hubert/shim.py, skeleton/registry (flat-file registration entry)
**Design rules:** BaseDevice/BaseShim inheritance required; no Postgres dependency in skeleton registration; log every lifecycle event
**Scope boundary:** Scaffold + registration only. No dev tool wiring yet (that is T-hubert-wire-dev-tools). No UI content beyond stub fascia.
**Completion criteria:** `python -m devices.hubert` starts without error and logs BOOT_READY; Hubert appears in /api/device/list response; stub fascia tab visible in web UI.
**Validation:** log.INFO('BOOT_READY device=hubert') visible in startup log.
**Observable via:** log.INFO('BOOT_READY device=hubert') at startup.
